title: CROWDAQ stream-level error event
description: Emitted when the backend is going to drop the connection or degrade the stream. The widget should flag the status pill (`reconnecting` or `disconnected`) and rely on the browser's native EventSource reconnect. This is distinct from HTTP 4xx/5xx responses on the initial open (those are described in openapi.yaml).

code.description: Short machine-readable code. Same top of the enum as the HTTP error schema in openapi.yaml, with two stream-only additions.

message.description: Human-readable explanation. Not for end users.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 UTC time the error was emitted.

retry_after_ms.description: Backend's hint for how long to wait before reconnecting. Widget does not enforce this; EventSource retry interval is driven by the browser.

examples.message: Bearer token expired; please reopen the stream with a fresh JWT.
Backend pod restarting for a rolling deploy; reconnect in ~5s.
